data.title: All About Cookies awards best password managers for 2025, promoting top tools for online security.
data.description: In Delray Beach, Florida on September 18, 2025, All About Cookies revealed the recipients of its inaugural Editor's Choice Awards for Password Managers. These awards recognize the best solutions that aid individuals in safeguarding their internet accounts and shielding confidential data. Through this accolade, these distinguished tools have been highlighted for their exceptional performance in enhancing online security.

data.title: Halloween House Freehold at Freehold Raceway Mall to open on Sept 26 with a unique Halloween experience for all ages.
data.description: On September 18, 2025, in Freehold, New Jersey, Halloween House Freehold is set to debut on September 26 at Freehold Raceway Mall. This spooky attraction promises a unique Halloween experience suitable for individuals of all ages. With a fresh concept introduced for 2025, guests can immerse themselves in a completely reimagined Halloween adventure at the mall. Don't miss out on this exciting opportunity to celebrate the spookiest time of the year.

data.title: IDCA partners with MDS UAE in Persian Gulf; IDCA is global digital economy think tank. Largest system integrator in Middle East.
data.description: In Dubai, UAE on September 18, 2025, the International Data Center Authority (IDCA) announced a strategic partnership with MDS UAE, the biggest system integrator and solution provider in the Middle East. IDCA, a prominent global digital economy think tank, aims to collaborate with MDS UAE to enhance their presence and operations in the region. This partnership signifies a significant milestone for both organizations, leveraging the strengths of IDCA's expertise and MDS UAE's market position.

data.title: "Augment Me introduces WotNow?!, 1st AI agent using neuroscience to enhance focus, productivity, and well-being in San Francisco, Sep 18, 2025."
data.description: A new application has been introduced using neuroscience and adaptive artificial intelligence in San Francisco on September 18, 2025. Augment Me, a cognitive alignment company, has unveiled WotNow?!, an AI agent aimed at enhancing focus, productivity, and well-being for students and professionals. The app focuses on engagement-based techniques to aid in these areas.

data.title: Akadeum Life Sciences' kit used to treat canine cancer by LEAH Labs & University of Missouri, first successful treatment.
data.description: In September 18, 2025, located in Ann Arbor, Michigan, Akadeum Life Sciences shared the achievement of successfully treating canine cancer for the first time. This milestone was reached through the utilization of their innovative Human T Cell Leukopak Isolation Kit to isolate cells. The groundbreaking work was spearheaded by LEAH Labs and overseen by Dr. Jeffrey Bryan, a prominent figure in the field.

data.title: LúmEnvy Skincare introduces Glow Between Ritual Kit with microneedling infuser, powered by Salmon DNA, in Syracuse.
data.description: Introducing a groundbreaking skincare set from LúmEnvy Skincare developed in Syracuse, New York, on September 18, 2025. This innovative kit combines clinical-grade components with an infuser inspired by microneedling, setting it apart as the first of its kind. Produced by PDO Max, a reputable medical aesthetics company recognized for its top-tier professional PDO threads and skin boosters, this skincare solution promises unparalleled quality and efficacy.

data.title: Kroger partners with first responders for 'Heroes Fight Hunger' drives in the US to support local food banks.
data.description: A well-known retailer, The Kroger Co. (NYSE: KR), has announced a partnership with first responders across the United States to commemorate Hunger Action Month. In this initiative called "Heroes for Hunger," they aim to support local foodbanks and pantries. This collaboration is set to take place in Cincinnati on September 18, 2025. This innovative approach highlights the importance of community involvement in combating hunger and the essential role of first responders in supporting these efforts.

data.title: Stock futures rise after Fed rate cut; jobless claims drop to 231,000. Nvidia invest $5B in Intel. Market projections positive.
data.description: U.S. stock futures rose on Thursday as major benchmark indices indicated higher levels. Initial jobless claims dropped to 231,000 for the week ending Sept. 11, less than the previous week's 264,000 claims and below the 241,000 forecast. The Philly Fed Manufacturing Survey indicated growth in regional manufacturing activities, with key indicators like new orders and shipments on the rise. Nvidia Corp. announced a $5 billion investment in Intel Inc., boosting premarket stocks. The Fed announced a 25-basis-point rate cut, with expectations of further easing due to labor market concerns. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.05%, while the two-year bond was at 3.52%. The CME Group's FedWatch tool showed an 89.8% likelihood of interest rate cuts in the upcoming October meeting. Stock futures change percentages were Dow Jones 0.75%, S&P 500 0.89%, Nasdaq 100 1.06%, and Russell 2000 1.50%. SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF rose in premarket. Most S&P 500 sectors ended positively, except for information technology and industrials. General Mills reported favorable Q1 results, while Manchester United shares dropped after mixed Q4 financial reports. U.S. housing starts decreased by 8.5% to 1.307 million units in August, missing the estimated 1.37 million units.
Vestand to acquire majority stake in AI Mindbot Equity in a new agreement.
Canadians Receiving Unemployment Insurance In July Rose 1.2%

data.title: FactSet Research Systems beat analyst expectations with $596.9M quarterly sales, 6.2% YoY growth. 8,996 clients as of August 31st. CEO highlights AI shift.
data.description: FactSet Research Systems (NYSE: FDS) announced its Q4 results, with quarterly sales hitting $596.9 million, exceeding analyst predictions of $593.5 million by 6.2%. The company's adjusted earnings per share for the quarter were $4.05, falling short of the estimated $4.13. Organic revenues rose by 4.5% to $587.3 million. By August 31, 2025, FactSet had 8,996 clients, a net increase of 185 clients in the past three months, led by corporate and wealth management clients. The Annual Subscription Value was reported at $2.406 billion. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Lennar Corp. to report Q3 earnings with expectation of $2.09/share, down from $3.90/share last year; Revenue $9.05B vs $9.42B. Annual dividend yield of 1.49%.
data.description: Lennar Corporation (NYSE: LEN) is set to unveil its third-quarter earnings on Thursday, September 18th after the market closes. Analysts are anticipating earnings per share of $2.09, a decrease from $3.90 in the same quarter last year. Revenue for the quarter is forecasted to be $9.05 billion, lower than the $9.42 billion recorded in the previous year. Lennar has exceeded revenue expectations in the last five consecutive quarters and in nine of the past ten quarters. However, in the second quarter, the company fell short of analyst predictions for earnings per share, breaking a streak of meeting expectations for the past ten quarters. Despite this, investors are keeping a close watch on Lennar's performance, with an annual dividend yield of 1.49%. More details can be found on Benzinga Pro.

data.title: Beacon Awards recognize top retailers in the hardware industry, with profiles featured in September Hardware Connection. #RetailExcellence
data.description: In Tavernier, Florida, on September 18, 2025, the recipients of the 2025 Beacon Awards, which recognize outstanding retailers, have been featured in the latest September edition of Hardware Connection. The awards were presented during the Independent Home Improvement (IHI) Conference's 13th annual event in July.

data.title: Upcoming Gastric Cancer Educational Symposium by DDF and Weill Cornell Medicine in NYC on Nov 1. Elevating stomach cancer care. (Characters: 109)
data.description: In New York on September 18, 2025, Debbie's Dream Foundation (DDF) will be hosting a Gastric Cancer Educational Symposium where clinical expertise and patient perspectives will be shared to enhance understanding and innovation in stomach cancer care. This event aims to bring together professionals and individuals impacted by the disease to advance knowledge and strategies. Featuring key insights and discussions, the symposium is set to address crucial aspects of stomach cancer treatment and management, with a focus on improving patient outcomes. Scheduled presentations and workshops will cover a variety of topics, including the latest advancements in research, available therapies, and patient support initiatives. This collaborative platform will facilitate the exchange of insights and experiences, fostering enhanced care solutions.

data.title: 10th Annual Participant Engagement Award is now open for submissions by Patient Enrollment Advisors and Cambridge Healthtech Institute for SCOPE 2026 conference.
data.description: The 10th Annual Participant Engagement Award winners will be revealed at SCOPE 2026 in Orlando. Patient Enrollment Advisors, partnered with Cambridge Healthtech Institute, has launched the award program and is accepting submissions. The deadline to enter is approaching soon.

data.title: 20% US IT pros say backup attacks main cause of breaches, 21% orgs fail full data recovery. Apricorn research shows.
data.description: A recent survey has revealed that 21% of companies are unable to completely retrieve data from backups, indicating significant deficiencies in their recovery plans. The survey was conducted in Poway, California on September 18, 2025. Apricorn, a prominent maker of software-free, 256-bit AES XTS hardware-encrypted USB drives, shared these results.

data.title: Gen Z parents prioritize preparing children for 'real world'; 54% seek to shift from 'gentle parenting'.
data.description: According to recent research by Kiddie Academy® Educational Child Care, there is a noticeable shift among Gen Z parents towards moving away from 'gentle parenting'. The study, released in Baltimore on September 18, 2025, during the hectic back-to-school period, reveals that younger parents are expressing a preference for a more hands-on and practical approach when it comes to preparing their children for the realities of the world. This change is significant as it shows a growing number of parents, with numbers estimated in the thousands, are seeking a more proactive parenting style rather than the traditionally advocated 'gentle parenting' methods.

data.title: Girl Rising's RISE Curriculum empowers Indian adolescents like Kusum, aged 17, to dream bigger.
data.description: In New York on September 18, 2025, a 17-year-old named Kusum Kaushik came across the Girl Rising comic book titled "Kusum's Adventurous Innings," which is part of the RISE curriculum. This comic resonated with her deeply as she found a reflection of herself within the character portrayed. The storyline follows a girl's relentless pursuit of education despite facing various challenges.

data.title: Advanced Lateral Flow Conference prepares for 2025 event with visionary keynotes; agenda announced.
data.description: A significant diagnostics event in LA JOLLA, California, on September 18, 2025, highlighted visionary keynotes and industry insights. The Advanced Lateral Flow Conference (ALFC), a leading global event for rapid diagnostics, unveiled its comprehensive agenda for 2025. The conference promises to offer valuable information and networking opportunities to its attendees.
